benchmark: vectordbbench-style head-to-head: pg_turbovec vs pgvector HNSW

corpus.name: Cohere/wikipedia-22-12-en-embeddings
corpus.note: Standard VectorDBBench 'Medium' size (1M). Real embeddings (not synthetic). Corpus rows verified distinct.

host.name: meh
host.simd: avx, sse4_1, sse4_2 -- NO avx2, NO avx512

software.pg_turbovec_ext_catalog: 1.7.1 (binary v1.8.0 .so loaded; scan path is in the binary; SQL surface unchanged for the index AM)

ground_truth.method: brute-force exact top-10 by cosine distance over all 1,000,000 corpus rows
ground_truth.engine: BLAS matmul (numpy/openblas), cross-checked against in-DB seqscan (enable_indexscan=off) for 3 sampled queries -> 10/10 overlap each; reconstructed corpus byte-identical to DB (max|diff|=0.0 on 4 sampled ids)

correctness_gate.small_table: 10k x 128-d distinct random unit vectors, turbovec 4-bit index
correctness_gate.note: Confirms the pre-AVX2 wrong-results bug (present in the old turbovec v0.7.0 / pg_turbovec v1.7.1 build that scored 0.0 recall on this exact host) is FIXED in the v1.8.0 build.

latency_basis_note: pgvector HNSW latency = server-side Execution Time from EXPLAIN (ANALYZE). pg_turbovec latency = client wall over a unix socket; the in-engine scan dominates (>40s) so the sub-ms cast/RTT term is negligible. NOTE: pg_turbovec latencies on this host are the SCALAR-FALLBACK FLOOR, not representative -- see caveat.

pg_turbovec_scalar_fallback_floor.reason: meh is pre-AVX2; turbovec takes the scalar score_query_into_heap path (correct but ~1000x slower than its AVX2/AVX-512 SIMD kernels). Per-query cost is a FIXED O(n_vectors * dim) full-corpus blocked-code scan -- independent of search_k, identical warm vs cold. On an AVX2+ host these are expected to be in the tens-of-ms range.

key_findings.correctness: recall@10 = 1.000 for pg_turbovec at every config (2-bit and 4-bit) vs brute-force GT on 1M x 1024-d real embeddings. The pre-AVX2 fix in v1.8.0 is confirmed correct.
key_findings.storage: pg_turbovec 4-bit is 7.6x smaller than pgvector HNSW; 2-bit is 15.2x smaller.
key_findings.build: pg_turbovec builds 1.9-2.1x faster than pgvector HNSW.
key_findings.recall_quality: turbovec is a quantized full-scan (flat) index -> near-exact recall (1.000 here). pgvector HNSW trades recall for sublinear latency (0.849-0.979 across ef_search).
key_findings.latency: DEFERRED to an AVX2+ host. meh's pre-AVX2 scalar fallback makes turbovec latency ~40-70s/query, which is NOT a representative competitive number.

deferred.tv_latency_frontier: needs an AVX2 / AVX-512 host (turbovec's SIMD kernels). meh is correctness/storage/build/recall only.
deferred.corpus_10m: not run (budget; 1M is the priority standardized size).
deferred.competitors: VectorChord / pgvectorscale comparison is future work.
deferred.concurrent_qps: pgbench multi-conn throughput not run (single-conn QPS captured for HNSW; turbovec QPS dominated by scalar-fallback scan).
